data.title: Appier Group reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Appier Group press release (OTCPK:APPIF): Q3 Revenue of JPY11.4B.
  • Record gross profit of JPY 6.2 billion, up 26% YoY, with a high level gross margin at 54.1% and is expected to continue the upward trajectory through year-end

data.title: AmpliTech signals $50M revenue target for 2026 as rights offering supports ORAN 5G growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AmpliTech Group, Inc. (AMPG) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Fawad Maqbool highlighted "record performance in the third quarter of 2025 and the 9-month period ending September 30, 2025," citing quarterly revenue of $6.09 million, marking a 115% increase year-over-year, and a gross profit of $2.96 million with a 48.6% gross margin. Maqbool stated that "EBITDA positive of nearly $200,000, reflecting substantial improvement in operating performance and margin expansion. Net loss significantly narrowed to $188,000 compared to a net loss of $1.19 million in Q3 2024." The CEO attributed growth to the integration of 5G ORAN technology and momentum in low-noise amplifiers and 5G infrastructure systems, emphasizing strategic R&D investment and a focus on high-growth markets.
  • Maqbool described the rights offering as an "above market price shareholder-friendly rights offering priced at $4 per unit, providing growth capital for scaling ORAN product lines and expanding domestic production." He explained, "The company increased revenue guidance to at least $25 million for fiscal year 2025... We project positive cash flow from operations and profitability to be achieved in fiscal year 2026."
  • The CEO projected "fiscal year 2026 revenue will be at least $50 million, effectively doubling that of projected record fiscal year 2025 of at least $25 million," and detailed a strategy for 25-30% annual revenue growth through 2030, primarily driven by ORAN 5G agreements with two customers.
  • Maqbool explained that the rights offering provides "choice, fairness and alignment," as all shareholders can participate on equal terms, and noted proceeds will scale production, secure inventories, fund certifications, advance product development, expand go-to-market channels, and maintain strategic flexibility without taking on debt.
  • COO Jorge Flores added, "We do have a mixed list of 4G and 5G products and every single one of them carries a different gross margin. All we can say right now... is that at least we're going to be able to achieve double-digit gross margins on every of our products right now."

Outlook

  • The company increased its revenue guidance to "at least $25 million for fiscal year 2025," representing a 160% year-over-year increase, and anticipates "double-digit gross margins in Q4 2025 and 2026."
  • Maqbool forecasted "positive cash flow from operations and profitability to be achieved in fiscal year 2026, assuming continuation of current order pace and margin recovery."
  • Management expects "to receive follow-on orders from both publicly announced LOIs imminently as well as orders from new customers in 2026," and projects fiscal year 2026 revenue at "at least $50 million."
  • The company aims for "25% to 30% annual revenue growth through 2030."

Financial Results

  • AmpliTech reported quarterly revenue of $6.09 million for Q3 2025 and a gross profit of $2.96 million, with a 48.6% gross margin. EBITDA was nearly $200,000, and net loss narrowed to $188,000.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and accounts receivables totaled nearly $12 million, with zero long-term debt and working capital of $14 million as of September 30, 2025.
  • For the nine months year-to-date 2025, revenue reached $20.7 million, and net loss was reduced to $3.8 million.

Q&A

  • Vishal Mishra, Bard Associates Inc.: "So just want to -- I heard you right, which is that you do expect these rights to be traded when they're distributed, correct?" Fawad Maqbool: "Correct."
  • Mishra: "Do we know -- can you give me a more color on that? Is it like those margins, which has been agreed upon are like the gross margin like in the 20s, 30s, 40s or that's not something you can disclose?" Jorge Flores: "We do have a mixed list of 4G and 5G products and every single one of them carries a different gross margin... we are not going to be able to disclose a specific gross margin information on our products due to competitive nature, of course. But that's why we are saying that at least we're going to be able to achieve double-digit gross margins on every of our products right now."
  • Mishra: "I know historically, you've been close to 30%, 40%. Is that something historically you will be or like because these new products, they will be lower, higher or..." Flores: "As you could see from our results in Q2, in which we had a lot of onetime cost driver, we have recovered very handsomely in Q3 with over 40% gross margins. So we are, of course, going to keep allocating capital... to improve on our margins."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were positive, highlighting momentum and congratulating management, while seeking clarity on tradable rights and margin details.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, using phrases such as "we are turning the corner and getting close to achieving profitability" and emphasizing strategic focus, margin recovery, and growth capital deployment. During Q&A, management was cautious about disclosing granular margin figures but reiterated positive margin trends and operational discipline.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more upbeat, shifting from explanations of margin compression and investment costs to highlighting margin recovery, EBITDA positivity, and a strengthened growth trajectory.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue surged from previous quarters, with Q3 2025 showing $6.09 million compared to prior periods. Gross margin rebounded to 48.6% in Q3 from lower levels in Q2, which management attributed to onetime costs in the earlier period.
  • Outlook shifted from a focus on overcoming initial investment hurdles and cost absorption in Q2 to strong revenue guidance, improved margins, and positive EBITDA in Q3.
  • Management's confidence increased, moving from justifying short-term margin compression to emphasizing operational turnaround and future growth drivers.
  • Analysts' tone moved from probing on order fulfillment and backlog visibility to focusing on financial instruments and margin sustainability as results improved.
  • Strategic priorities evolved from aggressive investment for entry into Tier 1 markets to scaling production, leveraging rights offering proceeds, and capitalizing on newly established LOIs.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted that gross margin specifics for individual products could not be disclosed due to competitive reasons. The company also highlighted the need for ongoing operational discipline and efficient capital allocation to maintain margin improvements and meet growth targets.
  • On the strategic front, AmpliTech emphasized the importance of successful execution on LOIs, scaling production, and securing supply chains to deliver on forecasted orders and sustain growth.
  • Analyst questions focused on tradability of rights, margin details, and sustainability of recent margin improvements, indicating an ongoing watch on the company's ability to deliver consistent profitability.

Final Takeaway

AmpliTech Group's Q3 2025 earnings call reflected a robust turnaround, marked by record revenue, a sharp recovery in margins, and a strong focus on scaling 5G ORAN product lines. Management outlined a clear path to profitability and doubled revenue targets for 2026, underpinned by strategic LOIs, a shareholder-centric rights offering, and disciplined operational execution. The company positions itself for continued growth, emphasizing operational leverage, expanding product certifications, and maintaining alignment with shareholders as it targets durable value creation in the years ahead.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BIO-key signals $1M–$3M renewal opportunity in Q1 2026 as partner network expands
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BIO-key International, Inc. (BKYI) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Michael DePasquale reported revenue of approximately $1.55 million for Q3 2025, stating "roughly in line with revenue in the first 2 quarters this year," with year-to-date revenue slightly under $5 million. He attributed the $600,000 decrease year-over-year to "quarter-to-quarter variability resulting from the timing of some larger customer orders."
  • DePasquale highlighted a $140,000 defense customer order that shifted into Q4 and confirmed ongoing deployments, noting expectations for the large banking customer to renew a contract in early 2026 on a deployment with over 29 million users. He stated, "we expect that we'll see $1 million to $3 million in business and renewal in the first quarter."
  • The company enjoys "very high renewal rates in excess of 90%, meaning our churn rate is in the single digits," according to DePasquale. He described BIO-key's "lumpiness" in quarterly revenue as a function of large order timing and anticipated "a very strong" year-end finish, driven by channel sales efforts in EMEA.
  • DePasquale announced the final stages of a new marketing messaging roll-out and a major PortalGuard identity platform update, stating "Version 7... represents our most significant update ever," with general availability targeted for late Q1 or early Q2 2026.
  • A new FBI FAP 20 certified EcoID III fingerprint scanner was introduced for regulated industries, with initial volume orders delivered to defense and government customers.
  • DePasquale emphasized momentum in the defense sector, referencing two of the top four global defense agencies as BIO-key customers and described the company as "well positioned to capitalize on these growing defense budgets."
  • He expressed confidence about future growth, while reaffirming cost discipline, stating, "we have been able to reduce our operating expenses by over 10% through the first 9 months of 2025, while at the same time, expanding our global reach and suite of solutions."
  • DePasquale noted $3 million in post-quarter net proceeds from a warrant exercise, positioning the company "in a stronger position to pursue growth."
  • CFO Cecilia Welch stated, "Our Q3 '25 revenue was $1.5 million versus $2.1 million in Q3, down approximately $595,000 year-over-year, principally due to the large orders Mike referenced in Q3 2024 that we did not have in this quarter."
  • Welch reported "license fee revenue was $918,000 in Q3 '25 versus $1.4 million in Q3 '24" and "service revenue increased slightly to $268,000 in Q3 '25 versus $267,000 in Q3 '24."
  • She detailed an 8% decrease in operating expenses to $2.1 million, with a 13% decline in SG&A, but a 5% increase in R&D to support product introductions.

Outlook

  • DePasquale stated, "we expect continuing deployments in orders even this quarter and beyond" with the large banking customer, indicating a renewal "scheduled for early 2026" with a "potential for meaningful future additions."
  • He discussed expectations for "$1 million to $3 million in business and renewal in the first quarter" from this customer.
  • He projected a "strong close to 2025" and noted that, "as we expect...we are in a very, very good position from a financial perspective to be able to grow our business and actually overachieve our objectives coming into the new year."

Financial Results

  • Welch stated Q3 2025 revenue was $1.5 million, license fee revenue was $918,000, service revenue was $268,000, and hardware sales were $364,000.
  • Gross margin for Q3 2025 was 77% compared to 78% the prior year, and operating expenses declined 8% to $2.1 million.
  • Net loss for Q3 2025 was ($965,000) or ($0.15) per share, versus ($739,000) or ($0.39) per share in Q3 2024.
  • Cash as of September 30 was $2 million, with $3.7 million in current assets. Subsequent to quarter-end, $2.9 million in net proceeds from warrant exercises were generated.

Q&A

  • Unknown Attendee asked about the Bank of Egypt deal's recurring revenue and prospects for 2026. DePasquale confirmed "that is a growing deployment" and expects expansion possibly in Q4.
  • On margins related to the Raya partnership, DePasquale said, "Our gross margins on software are 90-plus percent, and so they remain 90-plus percent from a gross perspective."
  • When asked about ARR, DePasquale stated "our ARR base, including renewals on our traditional contracts...are in the $6 million to $7 million range right now."
  • Jack Vander Aarde, Maxim Group, asked about the decision to provide guidance and prospects for 2026. DePasquale replied, "we're pretty confident in our position right now...the pipeline now is pretty solid, and we feel good about that."
  • On cost controls, Welch stated, "we are just analyzing everything...just trying to make good decisions."
  • Vander Aarde inquired about profitability breakeven. DePasquale responded, "Absolutely. No question in my mind. I do see it."
  • Unknown Attendee queried about causes for flat revenue. DePasquale cited "the transition from third-party to BIO-key product" and a "onetime shock" in banking revenue the prior year, emphasizing "nothing here in this business other than timing that I am concerned about right now."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on recurring revenue, margin sustainability, and timing of large contracts, with a neutral to slightly positive tone, seeking confirmation of growth drivers and expansion plans.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing cost controls, pipeline momentum, and upcoming renewals, using phrases such as "absolutely" and "no question in my mind" when affirming paths to profitability and growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone has shifted from cautious optimism to increased confidence, while analysts remain focused on tangible growth signals and timing of key contract renewals.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language evolved from a lack of formal guidance in Q2 to greater confidence in Q3, with explicit references to a "$1 million to $3 million" renewal and expectations for a strong year-end.
  • Strategic focus has shifted further toward channel partner expansion and defense sector opportunities, with ongoing cost discipline.
  • Analysts' questions in Q3 focused more on the timing and scale of renewals, ARR, and profitability, reflecting increased scrutiny of growth execution versus prior quarter's focus on contract visibility and margin sustainability.
  • Management's confidence is more pronounced, particularly concerning pipeline size and the impact of the Channel Alliance Program.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited timing of large orders and renewals as primary sources of revenue volatility, with DePasquale stating, "our business may continue to fluctuate on a quarterly basis based on the timing of larger orders."
  • The transition from third-party to BIO-key branded products delayed productivity but is now "productive."
  • Analysts expressed concern over flat or down year-over-year revenue, with questions on the impact of product transitions and nonrecurring prior year sales.
  • Management emphasized ongoing cost controls and a growing partner network as mitigation strategies.

Final Takeaway

BIO-key's management highlighted a growing pipeline, strengthened partner networks, and high customer renewal rates as key drivers for future growth, while emphasizing that the timing of large orders continues to cause quarterly revenue variability. The company expects a significant renewal opportunity of $1 million to $3 million in early 2026 and remains focused on expanding its global reach, maintaining cost discipline, and launching new products to position itself for profitability and sustained growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Market Voices: Trump attacks JPM, Hoffman; Swiss trade deal; Mamdani
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks. 

  • President Trump said he plans to ask the Justice Department to investigate the relationships Jeffrey Epstein had with former President Bill Clinton, JPMorgan Chase (JPM), former Treasury Secretary Larry Summers, LinkedIn founder and Microsoft (MSFT) board member Reid Hoffman.

"Now that the Democrats are using the Epstein Hoax, involving Democrats, not Republicans, to try and deflect from their disastrous SHUTDOWN, and all of their other failures, I will be asking A.G. Pam Bondi, and the Department of Justice, together with our great patriots at the FBI, to investigate Jeffrey Epstein’s involvement and relationship with Bill Clinton, Larry Summers, Reid Hoffman, J.P. Morgan, Chase, and many other people and institutions, to determine what was going on with them, and him," Trump said in a post on Truth Social. 

"This is another Russia, Russia, Russia Scam, with all arrows pointing to the Democrats. Records show that these men, and many others, spent large portions of their life with Epstein, and on his “Island.” Stay tuned!!!" Trump added.

  • The Swiss government said it has reached a trade deal with the U.S. 

"Switzerland and the U.S. have successfully found a solution: U.S. tariffs will be reduced to 15%. Thanks to President Trump @POTUS  for the constructive engagement. The meeting @USTradeRep with Amb. J. Greer was productive," the Swiss government said in a post on X, adding that details of the deal would be released Friday afternoon. 

  • U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer confirmed the deal with Switzerland in an interview with CNBC on Friday. CNBC added that the deal also calls for Swiss companies to invest $200 billion in the U.S. by the end of 2028 and that the 15% tariff on Swiss goods matches that imposed on products from the E.U. 

“They’re going to send a lot of manufacturing here to the United States — pharmaceuticals, gold smelting, railway equipment — so we’re really excited about that deal and what it means for American manufacturing," Greer said on CNBC. 

Swiss drugmaker Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) has already pledged to invest $50 billion in the U.S, Greer noted.

Novartis (NVS) is also based in Switzerland. 

  • New York City real estate executives say they're seeing an increase in commercial real estate leasing in the city, despite the recent election of leftist state lawmaker Zohran Mamdani as mayor. 

“In our business right now, we are seeing CEO after CEO committing to the city,” said RXR Realty CEO Scott Rechler at a CNBC investment conference on Thursday. “We’re seeing a record level of leasing in office buildings. And it’s not just for next year, it’s for 2028, 2030, 2032."

Bill Rudin, co-executive chairman of Rudin Management, agreed.

“We haven’t seen any diminishment in meetings with brokerage firms,” Rudin said. “People keep saying, ‘any impact?’ No one has put their pencils down. No one is calling the moving trucks. Companies are expanding and taking space."

data.title: VolitionRx signals transformative period as new partnerships target $85M APS market and drive 32% revenue growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: VolitionRx Limited (VNRX) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Cameron Reynolds, CEO, announced, "2025 efforts for Volition have focused on commercializing our groundbreaking Nu.Q platform in the human diagnostic market, and we were excited this quarter to sign not 1 but 2 agreements, a research license and exclusive commercial option rights agreement for antiphospholipid syndrome, APS, with Werfen and a co-marketing and service agreement with Hologic."
  • Reynolds explained the Werfen deal gives the partner access to the proprietary Nu.Q H3.1 NETs assay and enables investigation of its clinical utility in APS management. He described APS as "a complex disorder of the autoimmune system affecting around 4 million people worldwide," with a "total addressable market of approximately $85 million annually."
  • Reynolds highlighted the co-marketing agreement with Hologic Diagenode for Nu.Q Discover, which is "providing tools to biotech and pharma companies and also to academic and government organizations." He noted, "We have seen strong growth in interest from our Nu.Q Discover services and believe this partnership with Hologic will further accelerate the expansion of Nu.Q services to a wider base of customers to drive revenue."
  • Reynolds stated, "We are very confident of further licensing deals with a range of large companies, and we'll update on progress as they are completed."
  • Dr. Jacob Micallef, Chief Scientific Officer, detailed the progress on the Capture-Seq project, stating, "We've recently submitted a paper on this describing an entirely new liquid biopsy method to analyze blood samples to find DNA from cancer cells," with the method "achieving an 18,000% enrichment and remov[ing] more than 99.5% or almost all of the background DNA."
  • Terig Hughes, CFO, reported, "Revenue for the third quarter grew 32% over the same quarter last year, coming in at $0.6 million. At this early stage of commercialization, revenues remain fairly lumpy and difficult to predict from one quarter to the next. So we will not be providing revenue guidance at this point in time."
  • Hughes added, "Operating expenses for the quarter were down 10% year-on-year and down 18% for the first 3 quarters, primarily reflecting lower personnel costs and lower research and development expenses."

Outlook

  • Hughes said, "We will not be providing revenue guidance at this point in time."
  • Reynolds reiterated, "We are very confident of further licensing deals with a range of large companies, and we'll update on progress as they are completed."
  • The company aims for cash neutrality, as Hughes explained, "One of our key financial goals is to be cash neutral, meaning income, including licensing receipts, matches expenditure on a cash basis."

Financial Results

  • Third quarter revenue was $0.6 million, a 32% increase over the same quarter last year.
  • Operating expenses declined 10% year-on-year for the quarter and 18% for the year-to-date.
  • Net cash used in operating activities was $3.6 million for the quarter, a decrease of 33% over the same period prior year.
  • Net loss was down 8% for the quarter and down 20% for the first three quarters compared to the prior year.
  • The company received $1.2 million from a registered direct offering and, subsequent to quarter end, raised approximately $6.1 million from a confidentially marketed public offering.

Q&A

  • Justin Walsh, JonesTrading: Can you provide some additional color on the size of the antiphospholipid syndrome market and how it compares to other potential Nu.Q NET applications you guys are looking at? Reynolds: "There are 4 million people who have APS. So it's considerable. It's not one of the biggest markets, but that's a very good one to start with. And the TAM, we've calculated is something in the $85 million to $90 million per year."
  • Yi Chen, H.C. Wainwright: Has the Werfen partnership made any contribution to the third quarter revenue? How do you expect the Werfen and Hologic partnerships to shape the top line revenue trend in 2026? Reynolds: "Werfen have not accreted to the revenue. They're in the process of validating it on clinical samples for clinical utility... As far as Hologic, actually, I can announce today, they've actually made their first sale."
  • Steven Ralston, Zacks: Can you give some sort of breakdown of the product revenues? Was there any like stocking for Fuji? Or was there some lumpiness in the Discover side? Hughes: "Yes, there is still a lot of lumpiness, which makes it a bit difficult to predict and then discover is very project-based."
  • Bruce Jackson, The Benchmark Company: Were there any upfront payments in those agreements? Reynolds: "Yes, there were in Werfen... there were upfront payments and ongoing payments from Werfen."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on market sizing, revenue contributions from new partnerships, and product line breakdowns, with questions reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone on commercial progress and cost controls.
  • Management’s tone was confident in prepared remarks, emphasizing strong partnership momentum and technological breakthroughs, but more cautious and measured in the Q&A regarding revenue guidance, expense control, and timing of future deals. Hughes stated, "At this early stage of commercialization, revenues remain fairly lumpy and difficult to predict from one quarter to the next."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, with analysts probing for greater granularity on revenue and deal structure, and management stressing discipline in cost management and partnership execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw the announcement of two significant partnership agreements (Werfen and Hologic), whereas the previous quarter had not yet closed such deals.
  • Revenue increased to $0.6 million from over $400,000 in the previous quarter, marking a stronger sequential growth rate.
  • Operating expenses continued to decline, with management highlighting deeper cost cuts and progress on cash usage compared to Q2.
  • Analysts maintained focus on revenue segmentation, market sizing, and deal structure; the strategic focus shifted to execution of multiple partnerships and technology validation milestones.
  • Management’s confidence in licensing and commercial scale-up was more pronounced, while reiterating caution on the unpredictability of near-term revenue.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged that revenues "remain fairly lumpy and difficult to predict from one quarter to the next."
  • Hughes noted, "To fully realize our ambition, we need to execute several significant licensing agreements in the human space and secure existing milestone payments in the vet space."
  • Analysts raised concerns about the timing and scale of partnership contributions to revenue, the ongoing lumpiness of product and service sales, and the sustainability of expense reductions.
  • Reynolds acknowledged, "You're not going to become a successful company just by cutting. You've got to deliver the deals and products, which we're in the process of doing."

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that VolitionRx's transformative agreements with Werfen and Hologic validate the Nu.Q platform and open new addressable markets, notably APS with an $85 million opportunity. Revenue grew 32% in the third quarter while operating expenses continued to decline, reflecting progress toward cash neutrality. Although revenue remains difficult to predict, the company remains confident in the pipeline of licensing deals and upcoming technology milestones, with additional partnership announcements expected to further accelerate both commercial and financial momentum in the upcoming quarters.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ardent Health down after guidance cut; Morgan Stanley downgrades
data.description:
Ardent Health (ARDT) shares fell on Friday after the hospital operator lowered its 2025 earnings outlook with its Q3 2025 financials, prompting Morgan Stanley to downgrade its stock to Equal Weight from Overweight.

Citing industry-wide cost concerns, Ardent (ARDT) slashed its 2025 adjusted EBITDA outlook from $575M - $615M to $530M - $555M, which, according to Morgan Stanley’s Craig Hettenbach, at the midpoint stands ~13% below the estimates at the beginning of 2025.

The revised outlook reflects “a sharp divergence from peers that have seen 5%-10% positive revisions,” Hettenbach wrote, referring to ARDT’s peers such as HCA Healthcare (HCA), which lifted its full-year revenue and EPS outlook last month.

With ARDT down more than ~27% this year, the analyst argued that “there could be value after the sharp sell-off if Ardent can meet rebased expectations, but shares lack a catalyst for a re-rating.”

He slashed his price target on the stock to $12 from $22, trimming ARDT’s 2026 EV/EBITDA multiple to 4.5x from 5.5x and citing its lower EBITDA estimates.

data.title: JPMorgan strikes deals to get paid for fintech data access - report
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) has signed revised contracts with fintech middlemen to get paid for most of the data requests third-party applications make on customer bank accounts, according to a media report on Friday.

The move comes after years in which firms traditional lenders and fintechs disputed over customer account access. Firms like Plaid tapped bank systems for free when a customer sought to use the likes of Robinhood (HOOD) to draw funds, for example. Morningstar (MORN), Akoya, Yodlee and Plaid are reportedly among the fintech intermediaries doing business with JPM.

Late last year, the Biden-era Consumer Financial Protection Bureau approved the so-called open-banking rule, requiring lenders to provide customer data to other financial firms for free. Banks sued to block it, and their challenge gained traction in May when the Trump administration urged a federal court to throw the rule out entirely.

After several weeks of negotiations, JPMorgan (JPM) backed off its initial pricing demands, ultimately settling on lower rates, people familiar with the matter told CNBC. The fintech intermediaries also secured concessions on how data requests will be handled.

“We’ve come to agreements that will make the open banking ecosystem safer and more sustainable and allow customers to continue reliably and securely accessing their favorite financial products,” JPMorgan spokesman Drew Pusateri said in a statement, CNBC reported. “The free market worked.”

CNBC noted that JPMorgan (JPM) and the fintech firms declined to disclose terms of their updated deals.

data.title: Activist investor Kimmeridge takes stake in Devon Energy
data.description:
Devon Energy (DVN) +2% in Friday's trading as activist investment firm Kimmeridge Energy Management has taken a 0.9% stake in the company, Reuters reported.

Kimmeridge owned 5.66 million shares in Devon (DVN) as of the end of September, which would make it the company's 14th-largest shareholder, according to data provider LSEG.

The 13F filing from Kimmeridge did not detail anything beyond the number of shares held at the end of Q3, and whether Kimmeridge has since increased or decreased its position.

Kimmeridge has been pushing energy companies for better governance practices and to prioritize shareholder returns over expansive drilling programs; it said recently it had acquired a stake in Coterra Energy (CTRA) and was seeking leadership changes as well as increased focus on its Delaware Basin holding.

data.title: Least shorted S&P 500 stocks in October
data.description:
Chubb (CB) retained its spot as the least shorted S&P 500 (SP500) stock as a percentage of float at the end of October.

Short interest in the insurer increased slightly to 0.68% as of October 31, from 0.66% at the end of September. The company reported its Q3 financials in October that easily surpassed the Wall Street estimates, driven by strong underwriting results, as well as light catastrophe losses and robust prior period reserve development. “Chubb remains a 'buy,' as strong Q3 results and robust underwriting dispel margin pressure fears, despite recent share price weakness,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Seeking Profits.

Chevron (CVX) took the second spot, with a short interest of 0.7% vs. 0.79% at September-end. The energy company comfortably beat Q3 earnings estimates, as worldwide and U.S. oil-equivalent production set quarterly records, helped by the Hess acquisition. The company sees sustained cash flow and profit growth through 2030, while maintaining capital and cost discipline.

Microsoft (MSFT) followed, with a short interest of 0.72% as of October 31 vs. 0.75% at September-end. MSFT shares have advanced 20.8% YTD amid strong cloud and AI momentum. For the period ending Sept. 30, the Satya Nadella-led firm reported revenue of $77.67B, which included $30.9B from its Intelligent Cloud division, which consists of its Azure cloud unit. Microsoft said Azure revenue grew 40% year-over-year and 39% in constant currency.

Here are the top 20 least shorted S&P 500 stocks as a percentage of float, as of October:

  • Chubb Limited (CB) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.68%

  • Microsoft (MSFT) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.72%

  • Mastercard Incorporated (MA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.76%
  • Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.76%

  • Apple (AAPL) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.78%

data.title: Wedbush’s Dan Ives: Investors who focus on valuation worries will miss transformational tech stocks in the years to come
data.description:
Dan Ives, head of technology research at Wedbush Securities, remains optimistic about the tech sector (XLK), (VGT), (IYW) despite market concerns over monetary policy and AI valuations.

In an interview with CNBC, Ives described current market nervousness as “shortsighted,” arguing that the tech bull market will continue for at least another two years.

The tech bull pointed to strong demand for media chips (SMH), (SOXX), (XSD), noting that “demand’s increased, call it, about 30% since June from what we see.”

He characterized the current environment as a “CapEx super cycle” that represents the early stages of a technological transformation, saying investors are only in the “second, third inning” of the AI revolution.

Ives defended the significant capital expenditures being made by major tech companies, explaining that “for every dollar they put on CapEx, they’re going to ultimately make 8 to $10 on that over the coming years.”

He specifically highlighted Meta Platforms (META), Oracle (ORCL), and Tesla (TSLA) as particularly promising investments, calling Meta a “table pounder” despite recent selloffs.

When pressed about potential losers in the tech sector, Ives acknowledged the need to be selective but maintained his broadly positive outlook. He pointed to infrastructure players and second-to-fourth derivative companies as offering significant opportunities, mentioning companies like Nebius (NBIS) and CoreWeave (CRWV) specifically.

Ives rejected comparisons to the dot-com bubble, describing the current market as “a 1996 moment, not a 1999, 2000 bubble moment.”

He emphasized that investors who focus solely on short-term valuations risk missing transformational opportunities, noting that “if you focus just on valuation, one year out, two years out, you missed every transformational tech stock the last twenty years.”
